events.title: Screen Time with Toronto Public Health
events.description: In this session, the benefits and impacts of screen time will be explored. Discussion will include looking at recommendations for screen time per age of the child (between 0 and 6 years of age) and how to model and manage appropriate screen time behaviour. Physical activity recommendations as an alternative to screen time will be reviewed.

events.title: From the Golden Age to the Modern Era: A Musical Odyssey of East Asia
events.description: This concert introduces the history of the yangqin and its diverse regional and folk traditions, guiding the audience on a musical journey across China through sound, storytelling, and performance. This performance will include selections from different eras and styles, along with brief introductions to provide an overview of the musical journey. events.title: Blocks Challenges
events.description: Join us on a Saturday morning for a building activity! Complete the challenges using Mega Bloks and Lego Duplo blocks. For pre-school and school-aged children and their caregivers. Drop-in and self-serve. Unfortunately, we cannot accommodate any large groups/daycares for this program. If you or your child lives with a disability, and requires an accessibility accommodation to participate in this program, please contact Accessibility Services by email accessibleservices@tpl.ca , or voicemail, 416

events.title: Scientists in School: Science Meets Art
events.description: Discover what happens when science meets art! Explore perception as you use shadows, colour, motion, and light to create art from science. You’ll make a puppet, a spinning top and a kaleidoscope, as well as design your own galaxy. “The greatest scientists are always artists as well!” (Albert Einstein) Please note: this program is suitable for children 4 years old and above with adult accompaniment. events.description: Find your "inner magic" with The Amazing Magic Mel during this upbeat, hilarious and interactive magical experience. events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Unconventional Materials: An Osborne Collection Exhibit
events.description: An exhibition of intricately crafted books, artworks and much more. The Unconventional Materials exhibit highlights the use of unconventional techniques and materials in children’s literature, featuring historical and modern examples.   Visit the Osborne Collection to experience our exhibit featuring items such as plasticine art by Barbara Reid, dioramas and panoramas, postcards and other ephemera, and hornbooks and battledores. Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.title: Mother’s Day Craft: "If Moms Were Flowers, I’d Pick You”
events.description: Children will create a one-of-a-kind Mother's Day keepsake by painting their own hands to create flowers on a personalized canvas.  Under the theme "If Moms Were Flowers, I’d Pick You," this program offers a fun and sentimental way to show love for moms, grandmothers, and special people.  This low-prep, messy-fun activity will be cherished for years to come.

We'll explore plotting and characters so you can improve your writing skills and craft amazing stories of your own. Zoom URL to access the online program will be emailed to registrants ahead of program date. This online program is suitable for children ages 6-12. events.description: Join Fay & Fluffy (stars of Family Jr's The Fabulous Show with Fay & Fluffy) as they share some of their favourite books and stories. Fay & Fluffy's main goal is to share the message of inclusivity wherever they go, cheering on all the kids who see them to be proud of who they are, loving themselves and being kind to one another. Drop-in. No registration required.

events.title: Builder's Club

events.title: Traditional Lion Dance
events.description: Celebrate Asian Heritage Month as the Chinatown Community Lion Dance Group performs the traditional Lion Dance with drumming, cymbals and a Chinese Lion paraded through the branch. This will be followed by a short talk to explore the cultural significance, and interactive stations for children.

events.title: Intro to Chess
events.description: Chess is for everyone! Learn the basics of the game including how to set up the board, how to move pieces, and how to win. All are welcome – no registration required

events.title: Girls Coding
events.description: This program introduces children to beginner computer programming and fosters the development of essential STEM skills. Delivered in collaboration with Macaulay Centres for Children, the initiative focuses on promoting educational growth and enhancing future employment opportunities, particularly for children in marginalized communities. Participants will engage in hands-on coding activities, make new friends, and have fun in a supportive. Space is limited, and advance registration with a commit

events.title: Henna Art Workshop for Children
events.description: Celebrate Asian Heritage Month and discover the art of henna, decorative designs which are created on a person's body, using a paste of natural organic henna. Observe and enjoy the henna application and learn about its history, recipe and traditions. Led by Henna Artist, Hina Yasir. Drop In. Space is limited. Please Note: Henna will be available on a first-come, first-served basis. The final henna application will begin no later than 2:55 pm. No new designs will be started after this time.

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Instructional Chess
events.description: The Chess Institute of Canada is bringing chess to life at your local library! Join us as we explore the ancient game of chess and the essential life-skills the game imparts. Come and take in a lesson on the great game or sit down and start playing!  Learn, compete, connect with your community through the game of chess. Open to all ages. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Lego Free Play
events.description: LEGO Free Play is a fun, creative program where children can build anything they imagine using LEGO bricks. There are no instructions or rules to follow, kids are free to explore, create, and play at their own pace. This open-ended play encourages creativity, problem-solving, and social interaction. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: Saturday Knit & Crochet Circle
events.description: Want to learn how to knit or crochet with a fun group of people? Looking for help with your project? You can also share your projects with others, teach or learn a technique, and enjoy great conversation. All levels of expertise are welcome. Bring your own yarn, needles, and crochet hooks. All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration necessary.

events.title: Painting the Seasons - A Pipa Solo Concert
events.description: Audience will have this wonderful and rare opportunity to appreciate the unique Chinese music instrument live performance. Internationally renowned Toronto-based Pipa artist Wen Zhao will perform a solo concert celebrating China’s rich musical legacy, with a traditional and folk music repertoire spanning across dynasties. Open to all ages. No registration required, limited to a room capacity.

events.title: 3D Printing Demo - All Ages
events.description: Watch a 3D printer in action, see samples, learn how they work, and how you can use them at TPL's Digital Innovation Hubs. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing and how to use the Prusa software. Registration will start 2 weeks before this class.

events.description: Chef Yumiko leads a hands-on workshop to make the popular Asian beverage, Brown Sugar Oat Milk with Boba. Chef Yumiko will also provide a brief history of Boba and why it's so popular globally.

events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Kimbap Making for Teens
events.description: TV host and celebrity chef Sang Kim will teach the basics of making Korean-style kimbap , exactly like you see them on your favourite K-dramas. Participants will roll and cut kimbap using ingredients prepared by the Chef and eat their own creations. A brief history of kimbap, its differences from the Japanese sushi roll, and eating etiquette will also be shared. Prizes are also awarded for best kimbap creations. events.title: Youth Hub: Japanese Fruit Sandwich Making with Chef Yumiko
events.description: Chef Yumiko Kobayashi will provide a brief cultural history about "Furutsu Sandos", or Japanese fruit sandwiches, then lead a hands-on demonstration and workshop to make "Furutsu Sandos” consisting of fresh fruit fillings, bread and whipping cream. All ingredients to be provided and prepped by the chef. events.title: Coding with Python I

events.title: Accessing the Wisdom of Nature
events.description: The natural world is a source of living wisdom. The stream has something to say. So does the wind in the branches, the moss on a wall, the path a crow takes across the sky. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Games
events.description: Drop in to the Youth Hub every Tuesday and Thursday to play video games. Play with friends, by yourself, or maybe someone new.  Tuesdays will be Nintendo Switch days and Thursdays will be PS4 days.  *NEW* Starting at 3:30 pm, teens can sign up for a time slot to use the consoles. If there is no demand, gaming will be awarded on a first come first serve basis.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, wher

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Teen Quiet Study
events.description: Are you looking for a quiet space to study after school? Join our quiet study hall at Locke's program room every Thursday for individual or group study. Tables and chairs are provided, as well as a variety of materials to take study breaks. This is a drop-in program for Teens, and space is limited.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Youth Hub: Dungeons & Dragons
events.description: Are you ready for an adventure? Join us in the Youth Hub for our weekly Dungeons and Dragons game. Delve into a fantasy world to fight monsters and discover lost treasures in forgotten places. New players will be taught the rules and basics of play. Participants can either bring their own 1st level character or use one of our prepared sheets. Game will be run using the 2024 edition of Dungeons & Dragons. Each adventure is run twice a week, once on Thursday and once on Friday; players can re

events.title: Throwback Thursdays: Great Movies on the Big Screen
events.description: Come join us for a radical retro rewind projected on the big screen. Whether you're a first-time viewer or long-time fan, these movies are sure to please. April 16: Meet the Parents (2000, PG) April 23: Rounders (1998, R) April 30: No Country for Old Men (2007, 18A) May 7: Apollo 13 (1995, PG) May 14: The Martian (2015, PG) * special start time! 5:30 pm May 21: Contact (1997, PG) * special start time! 5:30 pm May 28: Star Trek (2009, PG) All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Night
events.description: Look through our board games and play something new! All materials will be provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Friday Teen Social
events.description: Drop in after school on Friday's to have fun with your friends, meet new folks, build connections, and tell us about programs and services you would like to see in the neighborhood!

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: All skill levels are welcome! Chess sets are provided.

events.title: POV: You Found a Good Book
events.description: Not your average book club. Think less assigned reading, more good vibes, real talk, and stories that actually hit. No pressure, no quizzes—just snacks, chill convos, and books you might actually want to finish. Come hang out, meet people your age, and help pick what we read next. You might even find a book you don’t hate.  Drop in. Bring a friend. Or just bring yourself—we saved you a spot. The Hate U Give

events.title: Guildwood Chess Club
events.description: Join the Tuesday chess club at Guildwood Library , as we play chess every Tuesday starting on May 5 th . Players of all strengths and skill level are welcomed to participate in the club. Chess introduction lessons are available for new players that are new to the game. The program is for children ages 7+, teens, and adults . For More Information, please call the Guildwood Library at 416-396-8872. Drop-In. No registration required. events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: LivingWorks safeTALK
events.description: LivingWorks safeTALK is a half-day workshop that prepares participants to recognize when someone might be having thoughts of suicide and how to connect them to life-saving support. Learn practical skills to help someone keep safe and guide them toward further help.  Who should attend? Individuals aged 15 and up People who want to support suicide safety in their community No previous training or experience is required. All participants will receive a LivingWorks safeTALK certificat

events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Soundscape
events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. This year we are reading:  February 19 -  The Strangers by Katherena Vermette April 16 -  Big Little Lies by Liane Moriarty June 18 -  On the Ravine by Vincent Lam September 17 -  Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow by Gabrielle Zevin November 19 -  Small Things Like These by

events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around the Branch Tour
events.description: The Brookbanks branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry team to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Free program.  Registration will open up one month before the program date. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver.
